paper.title: The Illusion of Readiness: Stress Testing Large Frontier Models on Multimodal Medical Benchmarks
paper.claim: Frontier models post high headline scores on multimodal medical benchmarks but remain brittle under stress (shortcut behavior, hallucinated perception, faulty reasoning), and are not yet ready for reliable clinical use.
paper.framing: Models keep improving. This page tracks how the paper's own benchmarks look as newer models ship, on the paper's own axes, using the paper's own methods. It is not a verdict on the paper.
paper.readiness_caveat: Higher accuracy on a public benchmark is NOT the same as 'ready'. The paper's readiness bar also requires robustness under perturbation, appropriate abstention, and clinically valid reasoning, dimensions a plain accuracy score does not capture. (Point made first by Yishan Wong in his reproduction.)
paper.version_drift_note: Between the arXiv preprint (Sep 2025) and the Nature Medicine version (Jun 2026), the paper's own model set changed: the preprint's DeepSeek-VL2 was replaced by Claude 3.5 in the main multimodal-accuracy figure. Even the paper's baseline is a moving target, which is why re-running over time is worth doing.

claim_status.basis: Accuracy keeps climbing (we measured Claude Fable 5 at ~80% on VQA-RAD), but the paper's bar is robustness and clinical readiness, not raw accuracy, and that bar still isn't met.

contributors.name: Gu et al. (paper authors)
contributors.role: Original benchmark, open-sourced MIT harness, and the published baseline results.

contributors.name: Yishan Wong
contributors.role: First independent reproduce-then-extend on the public VQA-RAD subset via OpenRouter; established drift baseline and ran current frontier models.

protocols.label: VQA-RAD · full set · exact-match
protocols.provenance: Exact values from arXiv 2509.18234 Extended Data Table 1 (preprint). The Nature version's Fig 1b swapped DeepSeek-VL2 for Claude 3.5; exact published per-model values are being pulled from the authors' result CSVs. The Claude 3.5 entry below is approximate (read from published Fig 1b) and flagged.

runs.notes: Read from published Fig 1b; exact value pending authors' CSV. Preprint used DeepSeek-VL2 (45.68) in this slot instead.

protocols.label: VQA-RAD · 100-item subset · LLM-judge
protocols.scoring_method: semantic adjudication by gpt-4o-2024-08-06 (LLM judge), temp 0, reasoning=medium, via OpenRouter
protocols.provenance: Exact values from Yishan Wong's addendum repo (independent re-run). NOT directly comparable to the paper's full-set exact-match protocol, different slice and scoring. Kept as its own series by design.

protocols.label: VQA-RAD · first-50 subset · LLM-judge (StillTrueToday run)
protocols.dataset_slice: first 50 items of VQA-RAD test (HF flaviagiammarino/vqa-rad)
protocols.scoring_method: authors' exact VQA-RAD prompt; semantic adjudication by gpt-4o-2024-08-06 (LLM judge), temp 0, via OpenRouter
protocols.provenance: First numbers produced BY StillTrueToday (2026-07). Faithful to Gu et al.'s VQA-RAD prompt and Yishan's LLM-judge scoring, but on a different 50-item slice, comparable-but-not-identical to Yishan's 100-item protocol, so kept as its own series. GPT-5 here (74.0) is our drift/sanity check against Yishan's GPT-5 (67.0 on his 100-item slice): same ballpark, different items.

protocols.label: Robustness under stress tests (T1–T5)
protocols.dataset_slice: per-test subsets defined in the paper (Table 2)
protocols.scoring_method: robustness score in [0,1]; 1.0 = fully robust to the perturbation
protocols.provenance: Exact values read from the published Nature Medicine Fig 1c heatmap. This is the paper's core contribution and the real 'readiness' bar, not reproduced by Yishan (needs the copyright-gated NEJM/JAMA items).
